sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: DDR5: How Faster Memory Speeds Shape The Future
feed.summary: This article discusses the advancements and challenges brought by DDR5 memory, which doubles the data rate of its predecessor, DDR4. It highlights how DDR5's increased speeds and power efficiency enable emerging technologies like IoT and V2X autonomous vehicles. The article also touches upon the future of DDR6, anticipating further speed increases and new signaling innovations to overcome physical limitations.

feed.title: AMD Powers Real-Time AI Processing at the Edge for Revolutionary Canon Free Viewpoint Video System, Transforming Live Sports Broadcasts
feed.summary: AMD announced that its Versal AI Core series, with AMD AI Engine technology, has been selected by Canon for its Free Viewpoint Video System. This system is designed to transform live sports broadcasts by providing immersive viewing experiences through real-time AI-based video processing at the edge. The technology allows broadcasters to deliver unique angles and near real-time replays, enhancing viewer engagement and enabling new monetization opportunities for live event metrics.

feed.title: Accenture to acquire semiconductor engineering services firm XtremeEDA
feed.summary: Accenture has announced an agreement to acquire XtremeEDA, an Ottawa-based semiconductor engineering services company, for an undisclosed sum. This acquisition will bring XtremeEDA's team of 40 engineers into Accenture Canada, enhancing its edge computing and silicon design capabilities. The move comes amid a global chip shortage and aligns with government efforts to strengthen Canada's semiconductor industry.

feed.title: Optical KDP crystal made in space now on sale
feed.summary: Redwire has announced the first sale of its space-manufactured optical potassium dihydrogen phosphate (KDP) crystal to Ohio State University's Center for Electron Microscopy and Analysis (CEMAS). This marks a significant milestone for space commercialization, with the space-made crystals valued at approximately $2 million per kilogram due to their superior properties, including a higher laser damage threshold compared to Earth-produced crystals. CEMAS researchers will study these crystals to understand their atomic-scale differences and optimize future space-manufactured optical products.

feed.title: Chip manufacturers press for federal funding as bill flails in Congress
feed.summary: Semiconductor manufacturing giants Intel and GlobalFoundries are urging Congress to pass the $52 billion CHIPS Act, warning that delays in funding could jeopardize their domestic expansion plans. Intel has already postponed a groundbreaking for its Ohio facility, while GlobalFoundries states its New York plant timeline depends on government investment. The legislation faces hurdles in reconciling differing House and Senate versions, but proponents emphasize its importance for U.S. semiconductor leadership and supply chain resilience.

feed.title: HPE chief Antonio Neri discusses as-a-service, GreenLake’s impact and the critical importance of silicon
feed.summary: HPE CEO Antonio Neri celebrated the company's successful transition to an as-a-service model with its GreenLake platform, fulfilling a 2019 commitment. Neri highlighted GreenLake's growth, now managing over an exabyte of data for 65,000 customers, and emphasized the critical role of silicon in HPE's innovation, including its supercomputers and support for the CHIPS For America Act. He also touched upon the company's stance on societal issues, specifically supporting women's reproductive rights.

feed.title: HPE Is The First Big OEM To Adopt Ampere Computing Arm Chips
feed.summary: HPE has announced its adoption of Ampere Computing's "Mystique" Altra Max Arm server chip in its standard ProLiant rack-mounted server, marking Ampere's first major OEM server maker partnership. This move, showcased with the ProLiant RL300 server, signifies HPE's renewed commitment to Arm-based processors after previous cautious ventures. The decision is driven by the Altra chips' deterministic performance and competitive price/performance, particularly for cloud-native workloads, positioning HPE to compete aggressively against Intel Xeon and AMD Epyc processors.

feed.title: Xfinity Stream app Launches on Apple TV 4K and Apple TV HD
feed.summary: Comcast has launched the Xfinity Stream app on Apple TV 4K and Apple TV HD, providing Xfinity TV customers with a new way to access their content within their homes. The app introduces a redesigned, intuitive user interface focused on content discovery, including editorial recommendations and personalization. This initiative follows a previous collaboration between Comcast and Apple to bring the Apple TV+ app to Comcast's entertainment platforms.

feed.title: AMD and HPE team up to spur sustainable IT through environmentally friendly initiatives
feed.summary: AMD and Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) are collaborating to promote sustainable IT practices by modernizing data centers, focusing on energy efficiency and reducing environmental impact. They have developed supercomputers like the Frontier and LUMI, with LUMI being carbon-negative and powered by renewable energy, demonstrating significant advancements in sustainable computing. The article highlights the growing corporate interest in sustainable IT, driven by institutional investors, customers, and employees.

feed.title: Chip shortage drives up vehicle prices, cuts sales
feed.summary: The global semiconductor shortage severely impacted U.S. new vehicle sales in the second quarter of 2022, leading to a 21% drop compared to the previous year. Despite high gas prices, inflation, and rising interest rates, demand outstripped supply, pushing average new vehicle prices to a record high of nearly $45,000. Automakers like GM and Toyota experienced significant sales declines, and while some expect gradual improvement, the shortage is anticipated to continue frustrating buyers and limiting inventory for the foreseeable future.

feed.title: AOT Growth and Innovation ETF Guide
feed.summary: This article provides a comprehensive guide to the AOT Growth and Innovation ETF (AOTG), detailing its stock profile, holdings, performance, and key metrics. It offers insights into the fund's expense ratio, geographical and sector exposure, ESG scores, and comparisons to peer ETFs, serving as a resource for investors interested in this active, multi-cap growth strategy.

feed.title: Direxion SOXL ETF: How To Trade This Leveraged Play On Semis (NYSEARCA:SOXL)
feed.summary: This article discusses the Direxion Daily Semiconductor Bull 3X Shares ETF (SOXL), a 3X leveraged ETF on the semiconductor index. Despite SOXL being down 85% this year, the author advises against buying it immediately. The recommendation is to wait for signs of a bottom in the broader semiconductor index (SOXX) before initiating a position in SOXL.

feed.title: Micron Announces Availability of DDR5 DRAM For Next-Gen Intel & AMD Server and Workstation Platforms
feed.summary: Micron has announced the commercial and industrial availability of its DDR5 DRAM specifically designed for next-generation Intel and AMD server and workstation platforms. This new DDR5 memory is expected to dramatically increase system performance by up to 85% over DDR4, catering to demanding AI, HPC, and data-intensive applications. Micron has been a key player in the development of DDR5 specifications and offers support through its Technology Enablement Program.

feed.title: Chip Market: TSMC Sales Soar 44% in Another Sign of Resilient Tech Demand
feed.summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. (TSMC) reported better-than-expected quarterly revenue, signaling robust demand in the electronics sector. The world's largest contract chipmaker recorded NT$534.1 billion ($17.9 billion) in revenue for the second quarter, surpassing analyst estimates. This performance indicates that the tech demand is more resilient than previously anticipated, despite broader economic uncertainties.

feed.title: Intel’s Dave Guzzi On The Big Channel Bet, Emerging Technologies And GPU Strategy
feed.summary: Dave Guzzi, General Manager for Systems and Components Partners at Intel, discusses the company's significant commitment to its channel partners, driven by a massive manufacturing investment aimed at shifting chip production away from Asia. He emphasizes the importance of providing partners with secure supply chains and resources like Partner University to help them develop solutions with Intel's emerging technologies, including AI. Guzzi also highlights Intel's ambition to compete in the GPU market, offering partners more choice and leveraging Intel's extensive software development expertise.

feed.title: STMicro and GlobalFoundries to build $5.7B semiconductor fab in France
feed.summary: STMicroelectronics NV and GlobalFoundries Inc. announced plans to construct a new $5.7 billion semiconductor production facility in Crolles, France, with significant financial backing from the French government. This plant, specializing in 300mm wafers and FD-SOI technology, is projected to be fully operational by 2026, aiming for an annual capacity of 620,000 wafers. The initiative seeks to support the growing demand for chips in automotive, IoT, and mobile device sectors, aligning with STMicro's revenue growth goals and GlobalFoundries' 22FDX manufacturing process.

feed.title: Samsung Announces 3nm Process Node, the First with Gate-All-Around FETs
feed.summary: Samsung has announced initial semiconductor production using its 3nm process node, which incorporates Gate-All-Around (GAA) FETs, trademarked as “MBCFET.” This technology marks a significant advancement beyond FinFETs, offering substantial improvements in power consumption, performance, and area reduction. Competitors like Intel and TSMC are also developing their own GAAFET processes, indicating a major shift in transistor architecture to continue Moore's Law.

feed.title: To Infinity and Beyond – New Frontiers for Exascale Supercomputers and DOE National Labs: A Virtual Briefing hosted by TFAI and the National Labs Caucus
feed.summary: This article announces a virtual briefing hosted by TFAI and the National Labs Caucus on July 14, 2022, focusing on the advancements in exascale supercomputing in the U.S. The briefing aims to discuss past breakthroughs, current research, and real-world applications, highlighting the collaboration between the Department of Energy, Oak Ridge National Lab, HPE, and AMD. It features a panel of experts and members of Congress, including Representatives Chuck Fleischmann and Bill Foster.

feed.title: Intel To Hike Chip Prices As Semiconductor Giant Deals With Inflation
feed.summary: Intel has announced price increases across its product lines, including chipsets and microprocessors, by as much as 20 percent, effective in Q4 2022. The company attributes these hikes to inflationary pressures, rising material costs, and increased production expenses. Other companies in the semiconductor industry, including TSMC and various material suppliers, are also implementing price increases, suggesting a broader trend of rising costs in the tech sector.

feed.title: ProKidney (PROK) Stock Price, News & Analysis
feed.summary: ProKidney (PROK) is a clinical-stage biotechnology company developing cell-based therapies for kidney diseases, with its stock currently trading at $1.75. The company has a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating from analysts, with a target price suggesting significant upside potential. While ProKidney's earnings are negative, they are projected to grow, and a significant portion of its stock is held by insiders and institutions.

feed.title: Chip designers warm to U.S. bill despite big benefits to Intel
feed.summary: Major U.S. chip firms are largely supporting a legislative package designed to boost the U.S. computer chip industry, despite earlier concerns that the subsidies disproportionately benefit manufacturers like Intel Corp. The legislation includes $52 billion in subsidies and an investment tax credit, primarily aiding companies that both design and manufacture chips. While chip designers like AMD and Qualcomm initially worried about not receiving direct benefits, they have reportedly agreed not to oppose the bill.

feed.title: Rambus Spins DDR5 DIMM Interface ICs for Data Center Servers
feed.summary: Rambus has unveiled new SPD (serial presence detect) hub and temperature sensor ICs, completing its DDR5 DIMM interface chipset. These new chips, alongside their existing Registering Clock Driver (RCD), provide a comprehensive solution for DDR5 memory modules in PCs and data center servers. The article details the technical advantages of DDR5 over DDR4, emphasizing the role of these new components in optimizing performance, capacity, and thermal management for modern computing demands.

feed.title: Corsair launches 32-inch 4K 144Hz and QHD 240Hz gaming monitors
feed.summary: Corsair has introduced two new high-end 32-inch gaming monitors: the Xeneon 32UHD144 (4K, 144Hz) and the Xeneon 32QHD240 (QHD, 240Hz). Both models feature Quantum Dot IPS panels with extensive color gamut coverage, AMD FreeSync Premium (Nvidia G-Sync compatible), and VESA DisplayHDR 600 certification. They offer flexible purchasing options, allowing customers to save money by opting out of the bundled stand if they plan to use a monitor arm.

feed.title: TSMC: Life’s A Beach, And Then You Make Dies
feed.summary: TSMC reported strong Q2 2022 results with $18.16 billion in revenue and a 44.4% profit margin despite a slowdown in PC and smartphone sales, driven by high-performance computing (HPC) demand. The company is advancing its 3nm and 2nm process nodes, with 3nm on track for volume production in H2 2022 and 2nm for risk production in 2024. The article highlights TSMC's industry leadership compared to Intel's struggles and discusses the implications of the CHIPS Act for domestic chip manufacturing.

feed.title: INTL ETF Analysis: Dividends, Returns LSE:INTL
feed.summary: The article provides an analysis of the WisdomTree Artificial Intelligence UCITS ETF USD Acc (INTL) traded on the London Stock Exchange. It details key statistics such as AUM, expense ratio, and inception date, and outlines the fund's investment strategy, sector exposure, and geographical breakdown. The analysis also covers performance metrics and frequently asked questions about its holdings and dividend policy, noting that INTL does not pay dividends.

feed.title: Micron Ships World’s First 232-Layer NAND, Extends Technology Leadership
feed.summary: Micron Technology has announced it has begun volume production of the world's first 232-layer NAND, marking a significant advancement in storage solutions. This new NAND technology offers industry-leading areal density, unprecedented performance with the fastest I/O speed, and improved energy efficiency, making it suitable for data-intensive applications from client devices to cloud services. The innovation is expected to drive breakthroughs in diverse markets, including data centers, mobile devices, PCs, and artificial intelligence applications.

feed.title: Nomura Malaysia launches Global Shariah Semiconductor Equity Fund
feed.summary: Nomura Asset Management Malaysia Sdn Bhd has launched its Global Shariah Semiconductor Equity Fund to provide investors access to the rapidly expanding semiconductor sector, driven by global digitalisation trends and the rollout of 5G networks. The fund aims to capitalize on the increasing demand for semiconductor chips by focusing on high-growth potential Shariah-compliant companies with strong financials and intellectual property. This fund is the first of its kind in Malaysia, with Nomura Asset Management UK Ltd acting as the investment adviser.

feed.title: Steward Partners Investment Advisory LLC Purchases New Position in ZIM Integrated Shipping Services Ltd. (NYSE:ZIM)
feed.summary: Steward Partners Investment Advisory LLC has acquired a new position in ZIM Integrated Shipping Services Ltd. (NYSE:ZIM) during the first quarter, purchasing 9,268 shares valued at approximately $674,000. Several other institutional investors also adjusted their holdings in ZIM, while analysts have issued varying ratings and price targets for the stock. The article also details ZIM's price performance, financial ratios, recent earnings, and dividend information.

feed.title: Samsung warns of weaker chip demand for phones, PCs as people shop less
feed.summary: Samsung Electronics has issued a warning that chip demand from smartphone and PC manufacturers is expected to decline further as consumer spending decreases. This outlook, shared by the world's leading memory chip and smartphone maker, suggests that even the more resilient demand from server clients might see adjustments due to growing recession fears. While Samsung achieved its best April-June operating profit since 2018 driven by strong server chip demand, its mobile business faced profit weakening amidst geopolitical issues, inflation, and rising costs.

feed.title: Opinion: Which Intel CEO is to blame for the current woes? Or is it actually AMD’s CEO?
feed.summary: This article discusses the blame for Intel's recent disastrous second-quarter results, particularly the significant revenue miss in its data-center business. It explores whether the fault lies with current CEO Pat Gelsinger, his predecessor, or the success of rival AMD CEO Lisa Su. The article highlights that Intel's results were so poor that analysts questioned why the company did not pre-announce them.

feed.title: AMD passes Intel in market cap
feed.summary: AMD's market capitalization surpassed Intel's for the second time, reaching $153 billion compared to Intel's $148 billion. This shift, while largely symbolic, highlights increased competitiveness in the PC and server chip markets and suggests investors may favor asset-light chipmakers. Intel's market cap decline followed disappointing earnings and a reduced full-year forecast, which the company attributed to execution issues and a slowing PC market.

feed.title: Newegg Studios’ “The Gamer Lounge” Challenges Video Game Players in Weekly Episodes
feed.summary: Newegg Commerce, Inc. announced that its reality-competition video series, "The Gamer Lounge," will now release new episodes weekly on YouTube. The show features social media influencers and Newegg hosts competing in various video games, with viewers able to win prizes by predicting episode winners. This move to weekly episodes follows strong viewer interest since its debut in July 2021.

feed.title: UCIe™ (Universal Chiplet Interconnect Express™) Consortium Announces Incorporation and New Board Members; Open for Membership
feed.summary: The UCIe™ (Universal Chiplet Interconnect Express™) Consortium has officially incorporated and announced new board members, including Alibaba and NVIDIA, joining founding members like ASE, AMD, Intel, and Samsung. The consortium aims to advance the UCIe specification to create a universal and interoperable chiplet ecosystem, with the UCIe 1.0 specification already available for download. They are actively seeking new members to contribute to the development of future specifications.

feed.title: AMD Slips 4% In Aftermarket Despite Earnings Beat & Another Record Quarter
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) announced strong Q2 2022 earnings with $6.6 billion in revenue, marking a 70% annual increase and a new record for the quarter, driven by growth across all segments. The data center division, in particular, saw an 83% annual growth, generating $1.5 billion. Despite the positive results and an analyst's optimistic long-term outlook, AMD's stock slipped 4% in aftermarket trading due to concerns about a potential slowdown in demand and inventory build-up echoed by other chipmakers.

feed.title: Opinion: Take a bow, Lisa Su: AMD’s data-center business is a true rival to Intel
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) has made significant strides in its data-center business, now posing a serious challenge to Intel. Under CEO Lisa Su's leadership, AMD's server efforts have transformed from less than 1% market share in 2018 to reporting $1.5 billion in quarterly revenue, showcasing strong growth while its larger rival struggles. This achievement marks a substantial resurgence for AMD in a critical market segment.

feed.title: How Taiwanese semiconductors reign supreme
feed.summary: Taiwanese semiconductor manufacturer TSMC holds a dominant position in the global microchip market, making the world heavily reliant on its products across various sectors. The company's strategic importance was highlighted during Nancy Pelosi's visit to Taiwan, underscoring geopolitical tensions and a global race for chipmaking dominance. Both the US and Europe are investing heavily to bolster their domestic semiconductor production, while China seeks to gain access to Taiwan's advanced chipmaking technology.

feed.title: Meet Ross Freeman, Inventor of the First FPGA
feed.summary: This article introduces Ross Freeman, the inventor of the first FPGA, detailing his life from a Peace Corps volunteer to a pioneer in modern computing. It highlights his role in founding Xilinx and his radical vision for the FPGA, which transformed the semiconductor industry. The piece also acknowledges his enduring legacy through Xilinx's market dominance and the annual Ross Freeman Award for Technical Innovation.

feed.title: AXS 1.5X PYPL Bull Daily ETF
feed.summary: This article provides a comprehensive overview of the AXS 1.5X PYPL Bull Daily ETF (PYPT), detailing its profile, historical data, and comparisons with alternative ETFs. It covers essential information such as expense ratios, holdings, performance metrics, and volatility analysis. The ETF is noted as no longer active, with an inception date of July 14, 2022, and focused on tracking PayPal Holdings, Inc. with a 1.5x leveraged bull strategy.

feed.title: Russian weapons in Ukraine powered by hundreds of Western parts: Defense think tank
feed.summary: A new report by the Royal United Services Institute (RUSI) defense think tank reveals that more than 450 foreign-made components, primarily from Western companies, have been found in Russian weapons recovered in Ukraine. These components, despite export controls for some, were acquired by Moscow over years before the invasion. The findings highlight Russia's military reliance on foreign microchips and suggest that stricter export controls and enforcement could hinder Russia's ability to replenish its arsenal.

feed.title: Infineon, AMD Stock Prices Show Diverging Fortunes in Chip Industry
feed.summary: The semiconductor industry is experiencing a divergence in fortunes, with chip manufacturers serving data centers, automotive, and industrial sectors maintaining strong demand, while those in consumer electronics face slowing sales and excess inventory. This trend is evident in the stock performance of companies like Infineon, which saw gains due to automotive demand, and AMD, which fell due to a PC market slump. The article highlights that analysts are drawing a "line in the sand" between these two segments of the chip market, indicating a shift from past blanket statements about the industry.

feed.title: GlobalFoundries, Applied Materials and Ford to Co-Host CEO Summit to Secure U.S. Chip Supply Chain
feed.summary: GlobalFoundries, Applied Materials, and Ford are co-hosting a CEO summit in Washington, D.C. to discuss securing the U.S. chip supply chain following the passage of the CHIPS and Science Act. The summit aims to foster public-private partnerships to accelerate semiconductor manufacturing, support automotive electrification, and strengthen U.S. economy and national security. Business leaders and senior government officials will explore how new investments can advance technology leadership and create jobs in the semiconductor industry.

feed.title: Are Semiconductors Set to Soar?
feed.summary: The semiconductor sector, a leading indicator for the broader stock market, has experienced a significant correction in 2022 due to supply chain constraints and an eventual potential supply glut. However, the recent passage of the CHIPS Act, which subsidizes U.S.-made semiconductor chips, could provide a significant boost to the sector. Direxion offers leveraged ETFs like SOXS for bearish outlooks and SOXL for bullish outlooks, tracking 300% of the daily inverse or performance of the ICE Semiconductor Index, respectively.

feed.title: Microchip Expands Portfolio of MPU-based SOMs
feed.summary: Microchip Technology Inc. has expanded its portfolio of microprocessor system-on-modules (SOMs) with the SAM9X60D1G-SOM, designed to ease the transition from microcontrollers to microprocessors and reduce design complexities for developers. This small-form-factor SOM integrates the MPU and DDR, along with power supplies, clocks, and memory storage, providing a streamlined solution for embedded applications. The SAM9X60D1G-SOM offers substantial memory, simplified power design, and customizable security features, targeting diverse applications like medical equipment, automotive telematics, and industrial control.

feed.title: Nvidia is hit by another ‘crypto hangover,’ and that isn’t the only problem
feed.summary: Nvidia is facing a "crypto hangover" and other issues as the pandemic-era booms in PC sales, videogames, and cryptocurrency decline. The company warned of a $1.4 billion revenue shortfall due to weak gaming equipment sales and excess inventory. Nvidia's stock fell 6.3% on the news, reflecting broader concerns about multiple challenges impacting its core business.

feed.title: Nvidia warns of lower second-quarter revenue on gaming weakness
feed.summary: Nvidia Corp warned of a 19% drop in second-quarter revenue from the prior quarter due to weakness in its gaming business, sending its shares down by about 8%. This follows similar warnings from other chipmakers and is attributed to consumers weighing purchases of discretionary items amidst economic concerns, as well as continued supply chain issues. The company expects to report overall Q2 revenue of approximately $6.70 billion, significantly below analyst estimates.

feed.title: Biden signs bill to boost U.S. semiconductor chip production, compete with China
feed.summary: President Biden signed a landmark bill providing $52.7 billion in subsidies for U.S. semiconductor production and research, aiming to strengthen domestic manufacturing and enhance competitiveness with China. The legislation, which includes a 25% investment tax credit for chip plants, seeks to address persistent chip shortages affecting various industries and boost U.S. scientific research. Despite some criticism regarding aid to profitable companies, the White House highlighted new investments spurred by the bill from companies like Qualcomm and Micron.

feed.title: Chipmakers tumble on fears of worst downturn in 10 years
authors: Jeran Wittenstein and Ian King and Bloomberg
feed.summary: Semiconductor stocks experienced a significant downturn following Micron Technology Inc.'s warning of slowing demand, raising concerns about the industry heading into a prolonged slump. Analysts believe the sector is entering its worst downturn in a decade, affecting every company and end market. Despite recent shortages and investments, the rapid evaporation of demand has taken experts by surprise, with some companies like Micron already cutting future spending.

feed.title: Beelink SER5 Review – An AMD Ryzen 5 5600H mini PC tested with Windows 11, Ubuntu 22.04
feed.summary: This article reviews the Beelink SER5 mini PC, equipped with an AMD Ryzen 5 5600H processor, 32GB DDR4 RAM, and a 500GB NVMe SSD. The author details the hardware, installation processes, and performance benchmarks for both Windows 11 and Ubuntu 22.04. The review concludes that while the SER5 offers very good performance, it should be considered an alternative to the SER4 rather than a significant upgrade due to similar performance metrics, with Ubuntu often outperforming Windows in gaming.

feed.title: AMD Stock Is a Bargain
feed.summary: Morningstar considers AMD stock a bargain with a fair value estimate of $130 per share, giving it a 4-star rating and a narrow economic moat. The company is praised for its impressive growth, market share gains against Intel, and strong execution in new chip designs, especially in the data center and PC markets. However, high uncertainty remains due to the cyclical semiconductor industry and AMD's smaller scale compared to Intel and Nvidia.

feed.title: Thematic Times: Semiconductors Power Thematic Indexes
feed.summary: The article discusses how semiconductor technology drives many thematic investment indexes, particularly after the increased recognition of their importance due to pandemic-related supply issues. It highlights the impact of the newly signed CHIPS and Science Act, which provides significant subsidies to boost domestic chip manufacturing. The piece also explains how various thematic indexes, such as those for electric vehicles, streaming, gaming, and even space technology, are directly or indirectly exposed to the semiconductor industry and stand to benefit from these developments.

feed.title: Thematic Times: Semiconductors Power Thematic Indexes
feed.summary: The article discusses how semiconductor stocks are crucial to many thematic indexes, particularly after the CHIPS and Science Act. It highlights direct and indirect exposure of various thematic indexes to semiconductors and the long-term benefits of the CHIPS Act for the industry and end users. The growth of the semiconductor market is primarily driven by the automotive sector, data storage, and wireless communications.

feed.title: Desktop CPU Sales Lowest in 30 Years, AMD Gains Market Share Anyway (Updated)
feed.summary: Desktop CPU shipments hit a nearly 30-year low in Q2 2022, marking the largest year-over-year decline in Mercury Research's records. Despite the overall market slump, AMD significantly increased its market share and revenue in desktop, mobile, and server segments, setting new records for x86 processor share and mobile unit share. Intel, in contrast, reported a rare loss and delayed key products, indicating a challenging period for the long-standing leader.

feed.title: Newegg Rolls into CNE Gaming Garage Powered by AMD with Advanced Battlestations Gaming PC
feed.summary: Newegg announced its participation in the CNE Gaming Garage Powered by AMD in Toronto, where it will display a custom Advanced Battlestations (ABS) gaming PC. Attendees can enter a sweepstakes to win the high-value PC, built by Newegg's Buildeniac division and featuring AMD and ASUS components. Newegg Canada staff will also be on-site during the opening weekend to engage with attendees and discuss AMD Advantage series laptops.

feed.title: Report: Qualcomm plans to return to the server chip market
feed.summary: Qualcomm Inc. is reportedly planning to re-enter the server chip market four years after exiting it, seeking customers for a new server processor. This new processor is based on technology acquired from Nuvia Inc. for $1.4 billion and is being evaluated by Amazon Web Services Inc. The move aligns with Qualcomm's strategy to expand beyond mobile and boost revenue, potentially increasing competition for Intel Corp. and Advanced Micro Devices Inc.

feed.title: Intel shares new details about upcoming Meteor Lake processor series
feed.summary: Intel has revealed new technical details about its upcoming Meteor Lake processor series, which will utilize chiplet technology for desktop and laptop CPUs. These processors will feature five chiplets, with the primary CPU module manufactured by Intel and other vital components like the GPU and I/O module produced by TSMC. This chiplet architecture allows for greater flexibility in design, improved manufacturing efficiency, and better performance through 3D stacking with Intel's Foveros technology.

feed.title: Making AI Run At Any Scale But Not At All Costs
feed.summary: This article discusses the challenges and solutions for scaling AI applications, highlighting how companies like HPE and Lenovo are developing accessible AI products. It focuses on Anyscale, a company founded by UC Berkeley researchers, which commercializes the open-source Ray framework to simplify distributed AI computing and make it easier for enterprises to develop and deploy AI models without extensive infrastructure expertise.

feed.title: Corsair Reveals Bendable OLED Ultrawide Gaming Monitor
feed.summary: Corsair has introduced the Xeneon Flex 45WQHD240 OLED, a 45-inch ultrawide gaming monitor featuring a bendable OLED panel from LG. This monitor offers user-adjustable curvature up to 800R, or it can remain perfectly flat for productivity. It boasts impressive specs including a 0.03ms response time, 240Hz refresh rate, and 1000 nits peak brightness, making it suitable for immersive gaming and HDR content.

feed.title: Newegg Joins Intel Gamer Days with Deals on CPUs, Gaming PCs and Laptops
feed.summary: Newegg is participating in Intel Gamer Days, offering promotional sales on Intel processors, gaming PCs, and laptops from August 25 to September 4. The event includes livestreams, exclusive deals, and a sweepstakes to win a custom PC valued over $3,400, powered by a 12th Gen Intel Core i9-12900K processor. Customers purchasing eligible products will also receive complimentary game codes for Arcadegeddon or Call of Duty: Modern Warfare II.

feed.title: Why Supermicro for On-Prem Data Centers
feed.summary: Supermicro offers compelling advantages for on-premise data centers, enabling organizations to deploy the latest CPU and GPU technologies quickly due to Supermicro's close relationships with suppliers. The company provides a broad range of application and workload-optimized server and storage configurations, including liquid cooling options, to meet specific performance requirements and reduce TCO. Supermicro also offers comprehensive IT solutions, from single nodes to large clusters, with full assembly, testing, and rack integration services to ensure optimal performance and customer satisfaction.

feed.title: AMD Set To Become TSMC’s Second Largest 5nm Customer Courtesy Of Ryzen 7000
feed.summary: AMD is projected to become TSMC's second-largest customer for 5nm technology, primarily driven by its upcoming Ryzen 7000 series processors. These new CPUs, including desktop, notebook, and datacenter variants, will extensively utilize TSMC's 5nm process node. This increased demand will solidify AMD's position as a major client for the Taiwanese chip manufacturer, second only to Apple.

feed.title: AMD Launches Ryzen 7000 Series Desktop Processors with “Zen 4” Architecture: the Fastest Core in Gaming¹
feed.summary: AMD has officially launched its Ryzen 7000 Series Desktop processors, featuring the new "Zen 4" architecture. These processors, built on a 5nm process, offer significant performance improvements for gaming and content creation, including up to 16 cores and 32 threads. Alongside the new CPUs, AMD introduced the Socket AM5 platform, which supports DDR5 memory and PCIe 5.0, and unveiled AMD EXPO™ technology for advanced DDR5 memory overclocking.

feed.title: Details Emerge On Nvidia’s “Grace” Arm CPU
feed.summary: Nvidia recently revealed new details about its "Grace" Arm server CPU at the Hot Chips 34 conference, confirming it will use Armv9 specification cores and SVE2 vector math units. The article explores the potential design, core count, and the decision to use LPDDR5 memory, highlighting Grace's integration into Grace-Grace or Grace-Hopper superchip complexes and its performance estimates for memory-intensive AI workloads like GPT-3.

feed.title: AMD launching what it claims is ‘fastest processer in the world for gaming’ in September
feed.summary: AMD announced the launch of its new Ryzen 7000 series gaming processors, touted as the "fastest processor in the world for gaming" by CEO Lisa Su. The processors are based on a 5-nanometer Zen 4 core architecture. This release comes amidst a challenging period for PC sales, with the low-end Ryzen 5 7600x model priced at $299.

feed.title: How to Get Windows 11 on HP Computers
feed.summary: This article provides a comprehensive guide on how to upgrade HP computers to Windows 11. It covers the benefits of upgrading, the essential system requirements, and step-by-step instructions for installation via Windows Update or the Windows 11 Installation Assistant. Additionally, it offers advice on preparing the computer for installation, backing up files, and troubleshooting common issues after the upgrade.

feed.title: U.S. Restricts Sales of Sophisticated Chips to China and Russia
feed.summary: The Biden administration has imposed new restrictions on the sale of sophisticated computer chips, specifically high-end graphics processing units (GPUs), to China and Russia. This move, affecting companies like Nvidia and Advanced Micro Devices, aims to prevent These chips, crucial for supercomputers and artificial intelligence used in applications like weapons development and intelligence gathering, from being used for military purposes. Nvidia estimates this restriction will impact approximately $400 million in revenue.

feed.title: Rising Contract Pricing Offsets Falling Demand, Total 2Q22 NAND Flash Revenue Increases 1.1% QoQ, Says TrendForce
feed.summary: Despite sluggish consumer demand and a decrease in bit shipments, the total NAND Flash industry revenue rose by 1.1% QoQ in 2Q22 to US$18.12 billion, primarily driven by a 3-8% increase in contract pricing due to a Kioxia raw material contamination incident. Enterprise SSD purchasing demonstrated strong momentum, offsetting weaker demand in consumer segments. However, TrendForce predicts a challenging 3Q22 with expected price drops of 13-18% and an overall revenue decline of 10% QoQ due to oversupply and aggressive destocking.

feed.title: AMD says U.S. told it to stop shipping top AI chip to China
feed.summary: AMD announced on Wednesday that U.S. officials instructed them to cease exporting their top artificial intelligence MI250 chips to China due to new license requirements. While MI100 chips are reportedly unaffected, AMD does not anticipate a material impact on its business. This news follows a similar announcement from rival Nvidia, which projected a potential $400 million loss in sales due to the new regulations.

feed.title: Nvidia’s Red Scare, Costco’s Going “There” & No Campbell’s Soup For You!
feed.summary: Nvidia experienced a significant stock drop after the U.S. Commerce Department restricted the sale of its AI-related chips to China and Russia, citing national security concerns, which is expected to cost the company $400 million quarterly. Meanwhile, Costco is rumored to be considering a membership fee increase following a similar move by Sam's Club, and Campbell's reported earnings highlighted the impact of inflation on food production costs. The article also touches on Micron's investment in a new chipmaking plant and Signet Jewelers' mixed quarterly results amidst inflation fears.

feed.title: Nvidia says U.S. government allows A.I. chip development in China
feed.summary: Nvidia confirmed that the U.S. government has permitted it to continue developing its H100 artificial intelligence chip in China, despite new export restrictions on high-performance AI chips to the region. The restrictions, which still ban sales of the A100 and H100 chips to China and Russia, are expected to cost Nvidia $400 million in revenue for the current quarter. Developing the H100 in China is allowed, and Nvidia can ship existing AI chips from Hong Kong until September 2023.

feed.title: Arm sues Qualcomm, wants it to “destroy” Nuvia’s pre-acquisition CPU designs
feed.summary: Arm is suing Qualcomm over its $1.4 billion acquisition of Nuvia, claiming the purchase caused Nuvia to breach its Arm licenses. Arm alleges that Qualcomm and Nuvia continued to develop Arm-based technology under the terminated licenses without consent, demanding that they stop using and destroy the relevant Nuvia technology. Qualcomm denies these claims, asserting it has broad licensing rights covering its custom-designed CPUs.

feed.title: U.S. officials order Nvidia to halt sales of top AI chips to China
feed.summary: U.S. officials have ordered Nvidia to cease exporting its top AI chips, the A100 and H100, to China, significantly escalating the U.S. crackdown on China's technological advancements. This move, aimed at preventing the chips from being used for military purposes, could hinder Chinese firms' ability to perform advanced AI work and impact Nvidia's future sales in the region. Rival AMD also received similar restrictions on its MI250 chips, but stated the impact on its business would not be material.

feed.title: New U.S. curbs on sales of Nvidia AI chips to China spark selloff
feed.summary: New U.S. restrictions on exports of advanced AI chips from Nvidia and AMD to China have caused Nvidia's stock to fall over 11%. The curbs, aimed at limiting China's technological prowess, specifically target Nvidia's H100 and A100 chips which are crucial for AI and machine learning applications. While potentially impacting Nvidia's business in China, the U.S. government has made some allowances for the completion and support of the H100 chip.

feed.title: Arm sues Qualcomm, aiming to unwind Qualcomm's $1.4 bln Nuvia purchase
feed.summary: Arm Ltd. has sued Qualcomm Inc. and its recently acquired chip design firm Nuvia Inc. for breach of license agreements and trademark infringement, seeking to unwind Qualcomm's $1.4 billion Nuvia purchase. Arm is requesting an injunction that would compel Qualcomm to destroy designs developed under Nuvia’s license agreements with Arm, alleging its approval was needed before the transfer to Qualcomm. This lawsuit signifies a major rift between two key technology partners, as Qualcomm had acquired Nuvia to boost its custom computing core designs and challenge competitors in the PC market.

feed.title: STMicroelectronics Introduces Stellar P6 Automotive MCU for EV Platform System Integration
feed.summary: STMicroelectronics has introduced its new Stellar P6 automotive microcontrollers, designed for next-generation electric vehicles. These MCUs support electrified drivetrains and domain-oriented, over-the-air updateable systems, integrating the new CAN-XL in-car communication standard. The Stellar P6 aims to facilitate the transition to software-defined vehicles, handling growing data flows and ensuring secure, real-time performance and energy management.

feed.title: Taiwan chip magnate pledges cash for defense against China: 'I'm telling everyone to oppose the CCP'
feed.summary: Robert Tsao, founder of Taiwan's first semiconductor company UMC, has pledged $32 million of his personal wealth to fund the Kuma Academy, which trains Taiwan's civilian defense corps. This initiative aims to train over three million "civilian warriors" and sharpshooters annually to defend against a potential Chinese invasion. Tsao, who has regained Taiwanese citizenship after giving it up, stated his strong opposition to the Chinese Communist Party, a stance he adopted after witnessing the crackdown on pro-democracy supporters in Hong Kong.

feed.title: Skyworks (SWKS) Unveils Network Synchronization Solutions
feed.summary: Skyworks Solutions (SWKS) has launched new network synchronization solutions, including Si551x and Si540x products and AccuTime IEEE 1588 software, to support 5G fronthaul networks with precise time synchronization. These solutions, developed in partnership with NXP Semiconductors and Advanced Micro Devices, are crucial for emerging 5G applications and the IoT. Despite a challenging macroeconomic environment and competition, Skyworks aims to leverage organic growth and an expanding product portfolio to capitalize on 5G and IoT megatrends.

feed.title: Meta, Qualcomm sign pact on custom virtual reality chips | Hindustan Times
feed.summary: Meta Platforms Inc. and Qualcomm Inc. have signed an agreement for Qualcomm to produce custom chipsets for Meta's Quest virtual reality (VR) devices. This collaboration highlights Meta's reliance on Qualcomm's technology while it also develops its own custom silicon for its VR, augmented, and mixed reality devices. The financial terms of the deal were not disclosed, and the custom chipsets will be optimized for Quest's system specifications, though not exclusive to Meta.

feed.title: Game on: Why esports and gaming could take it to the next level
feed.summary: The esports and gaming sector, a multi-billion-dollar industry, continues to grow despite recent slowdowns attributed to supply chain delays and shifting consumer choices post-pandemic. Analysts and industry experts are confident in long-term growth, citing upcoming title launches and easing parts shortages. Mobile gaming remains the primary driver of revenue growth globally, while esports is gaining mainstream traction with significant investment and increasing popularity in emerging markets.

feed.title: Chip Delivery Times Shrink in August
feed.summary: Global chip delivery times shortened in August to an average of 26.8 weeks, a slight improvement signifying easing supply shortages due to slowing demand for products like phones and PCs. Despite this, certain semiconductors such as power management and microcontrollers remain hard to acquire, and the industry struggles to match supply with demand, leading to concerns about over-ordering and inventory build-up. The Philadelphia Stock Market Semiconductor Index has seen a significant downturn, reflecting investor apprehension.

feed.title: Pentagon eyes commercial solution to supply chain problems
feed.summary: The U.S. Department of Defense is seeking commercial software solutions to address ongoing supply chain disruptions, which have been exacerbated by the pandemic and inflation. The Defense Innovation Unit (DIU) issued a solicitation for software that can build new supply pathways and manage risk for critical components, especially important given concerns about foreign reliance for microelectronics and recent issues like the F-35's Chinese-made magnet. The Pentagon aims to use analytical techniques to integrate data from various sources, create supply chain models, estimate costs, and identify risks.

feed.title: US-China chip war is overheating
feed.summary: The Biden administration plans to expand restrictions on U.S. semiconductor and chipmaking tool exports to China, targeting advanced AI chips and manufacturing equipment. These new regulations will codify earlier limitations imposed on companies like KLA Corp, Lam Research Corp, Applied Materials Inc, Nvidia Corp, and Advanced Micro Devices. The move is part of the U.S. strategy to curb China's technological advancements and strengthen its domestic semiconductor industry through initiatives like the $50 billion CHIPS Act.

feed.title: Supermicro Adds New 8U Universal GPU Server That Delivers Maximum Performance and Flexibility for Large Scale AI Training, NVIDIA® Omniverse, and Metaverse
feed.summary: Supermicro has launched a new 8U Universal GPU server designed for large-scale AI training, NVIDIA Omniverse, and the Metaverse. This server features eight NVIDIA H100 Tensor Core GPUs, offering maximum performance and flexibility. Its advanced airflow design helps reduce Power Usage Effectiveness (PUE) in data centers while maintaining high performance.

feed.title: Supermicro Announces 8U H100 HGX Server
feed.summary: Supermicro has announced its new 8U Universal GPU Server, incorporating eight NVIDIA H100 Tensor Core GPUs, designed for large-scale AI training, NVIDIA Omniverse, and Metaverse applications. This server increases AI performance with improved thermal density, enabling lower power consumption and operation at higher data center temperatures, while supporting both current and next-generation CPUs and GPUs. The company is expanding its leading GPU server lineup with this addition, offering flexibility with front and rear I/O, AC or DC power support, and liquid cooling options.

feed.title: Arm unveils updated Neoverse CPU roadmap, targeting cloud, hyperscale and HPC workloads
feed.summary: Arm has announced an updated Neoverse CPU roadmap to provide a foundation for various workloads in cloud, hyperscale, and high-performance computing. The new Neoverse V2, codenamed Demeter, aims to deliver top per-thread performance and power efficiency, with partners like Nvidia already integrating it. Additionally, Arm detailed upcoming N-series and E-series chip designs set for a 2023 launch, catering to different compute needs prioritizing thread count or data plane processing.

feed.title: Google and NIST Partner to Open Source Nanotechnology Testbed Wafers
feed.summary: Google and the US National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST) have partnered to create an open-source, wafer-scale testbed for nanotechnology research, built on SkyWater Technologies' SKY130 process node. Google will distribute these nanotech test wafers to universities and release the designs under an open-source license. This initiative aims to facilitate research, improve access to semiconductor technology, and streamline the transition of innovations from lab to practical application.

feed.title: Data, Data everywhere, but where to store it all
feed.summary: The world is generating an unprecedented amount of data, leading to concerns about running out of storage space. Current trends show data creation outpacing storage capacity, with Zettabytes of information being generated daily. The article explores technological advancements in hard drive capacities, such as HAMR and MAMR, and radical concepts like quantum computing and graphene storage, while also highlighting the practical challenges of implementing these solutions and the long-term environmental impact of data storage.

feed.title: Serverless Data Centers – Wave of the future?
feed.summary: This article explores the concept of "serverless data centers" by combining serverless computing and hardware disaggregation. It explains how serverless computing allows developers to focus on application code by abstracting infrastructure management, while hardware disaggregation breaks down monolithic servers into pools of specialized resources. The author argues that true serverless data centers, leveraging disaggregated hardware, will enable flexible and efficient resource allocation tailored precisely to application needs.

feed.title: NVIDIA Delivers Quantum Leap in Performance, Introduces New Era of Neural Rendering With GeForce RTX 40 Series
feed.summary: NVIDIA unveiled its new GeForce RTX 40 Series GPUs, powered by the Ada Lovelace architecture and new DLSS 3 technology, promising up to 4x performance improvement for gamers and creators. The flagship RTX 4090 and two configurations of the RTX 4080 were introduced, alongside new features like neural rendering and RTX Remix for classic games. These advancements aim to redefine real-time ray tracing, AI-powered graphics, and creative workflows.

feed.title: Nvidia’s new AI-focused chip produces more realistic images in games
feed.summary: Nvidia Corp. has unveiled its new AI-focused graphics chip, the RTX 4090, which is designed to produce more realistic images in games by combining AI with traditional pixel calculations. The high-end chip, costing US$1,599, features 76 billion transistors and 24GB of memory, and will go on sale October 12, with other versions following in November. This launch aims to give customers a reason to upgrade amidst a slowdown in PC component demand.

feed.title: Supermicro Delivers Second-Generation NVIDIA® OVX™ Computing System for 3D Collaboration, Metaverse, and Digital Twin Simulation, Powered by the New NVIDIA L40 GPU
feed.summary: Supermicro announced the release of its second-generation NVIDIA OVX computing system, powered by the new NVIDIA L40 GPU, designed for 3D collaboration, metaverse, and digital twin simulation. These new servers aim to provide the necessary graphics, AI, and network performance for building and operating immersive 3D virtual worlds at data center scale. The systems will support up to eight NVIDIA L40 GPUs, ConnectX-7 SmartNICs, and current/future CPU processors from Intel and AMD.

feed.title: Supermicro Delivers Second-Generation NVIDIA® OVX™ Computing System for 3D Collaboration, Metaverse, and Digital Twin Simulation, Powered by the New NVIDIA L40 GPU
feed.summary: Supermicro has launched its second-generation NVIDIA OVX computing systems, powered by the new NVIDIA L40 GPU, designed to accelerate 3D collaboration, metaverse applications, and digital twin simulations. These servers are optimized for NVIDIA Omniverse Enterprise applications, offering advanced graphics, AI, and networking performance. The systems support up to eight NVIDIA L40 GPUs, ConnectX-7 SmartNICs, high-performance CPUs, and NVMe storage.
